name: Re-verification
description: Run verifier on the evolved snapshot as-is; keep passing proofs, leave failing ones broken
notes: 'Lower bound. Status-quo behavior of current verification workflows."

name: Syntactic proof transplant
description: Apply old proof scripts and annotations to new version with rename-only, diff- aware textual patching
notes: Measures how much can be recovered by surface-level alignment alone.

name: Neural proof synthesis from scratch
description: TacTok/Passport-style tactic-level prediction trained on proof states; ignore old proof
notes: Tests the central hypothesis that repair beats synthesis when a prior proof exists.

name: LLM-only proof repair
description: Prompt an LLM (CodeBERT/GraphCodeBERT/InCoder/GPT-4) with (proof_old, diff, verifier_error to produce proof_new; verifier-only acceptance
notes: Pure LLM-APR analog. No symbolic alignment, no repair calculus.

name: APR-style generate-and-validate
description: Patch-template enumeration with search; replace test oracle by proof- checker oracle
notes: APR methodology adapted to proof artifacts. No cross-version alignment.

name: Symbolic-only ablation (ours minus LLM)
description: Full alignment + repair-calculus pipeline with LLM candidate generator disabled
notes: Ablation: measures LLM contribution; required to defend against Risk 3 (LLM adds little).

name: Equality-saturation rewrite repair (optional)
description: egg-based algebraic rewriting of goals/assertions, no learning
notes: Optional non-ML symbolic baseline; useful if scope allows.

name: Automatic repair rate
description: Percent of broken proofs accepted by the verifier after a fully automatic patch, zero human edits
notes: Primary quality metric. Applies to all baselines and all datasets.

name: Interactive repair rate @ k
description: Percent of cases where the user accepts a suggestion after at most k token edits (k in {1,5,20})
notes: Workflow-realistic. Used in human study for $13.3.

name: Patch minimality
description: Tree-edit-distance on AST / proof-script between the tool's repair and the nearest accepted version
notes: Operationalizes the 'repair minimality preference' property in $9.2.

name: Alignment precision and recall
description: TP/FP/FN on old-new entity mappings against manual ground truth
notes: Directly tests RQ2. Applicable only to baselines that produce alignments (B5, B6, Ours).

name: Candidate budget
description: Median and p95 number of candidates tried before a proof-checked acceptance
notes: Measures search efficiency. Symbolic priors expected to dominate LLM-only on this metric.

name: Wall-clock time to restored proof
description: End-to-end time including alignment + search + verifier runs

name: Brittleness vs drift classification F1
description: Correctness of the repair-vs-escalate decision, against human labels on mixed real-history cases
notes: Directly tests RQ4. Prevents 'repairing around' genuine regressions.

name: Backend robustness rescuing rate (optional)
description: Percent of solver-version-induced failures successfully repaired across Z3/CVC5 versions
notes: Isolates toolchain-side brittleness. Critical for D4 analysis.

name: Formalization developments corpus
description: Version pairs from formalization developments in Mathlib, Dafny stdlib, Verus, F* stdlib, SteelCore, MoSeL examples, and Equations examples. Each pair labeled with failing-to-fixed verification state.
notes: Target size ~2,000-5,000 pairs. Hardest to build but highest empirical weight.

name: Rust verification evolution corpus
description: Version pairs from Prusti, RustHorn, Aeneas, Flux, and lightweight- formalism examples where Rust compiler / lifetime elaboration changes caused proof churn",006;031;032;043;046,"Target ~500-1
notes: pairs. Tests evolution coupled with fast-moving source toolchain.

name: Controlled refactoring benchmark
description: Synthetic semantics-preserving refactorings applied to verified programs: extract-method, inline, rename, reorder, loop-reshape, contract-factor, ghost-state-restructure, helper-lemma introduction*, 002;077;078, Target ~1
notes: pairs. Oracle is known by construction: all should be repairable. Gold standard for RQ1 and RQ3.

name: Backend-variation benchmark
description: Same source and proof artifacts evaluated across different backends: Z3 4.8/4.11/4.12, CVC5, alternative SMT-LIB flags, Boogie encoding variants", 085;086;087,"Target ~ 300-
notes: triples. Isolates backend-only brittleness. Primary source for M8.
